questions: What principles guide rigorous causal measurement in marketing?
How should experiments and models be combined?
qa.answer: Use identification strategies that isolate exogenous variation (RCTs, geo tests, IV/natural experiments) and combine them with structural or aggregate models to generalize and inform policy. Calibrate models with experimental evidence to reduce bias.
implication: Anchor MMM/MTA on experiment results; publish calibration cadence.
Design tests with power and guardrails; validate assumptions.
Use counterfactual reasoning and sensitivity analyses for decisions.
